prose:
Method–provenance block
Property: Fukui reactivity indices (f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor) and most electrophilic/nucleophilic sites for aniline.
Level of theory: DFT, functional B3LYP with def2-TZVP basis set (tier ‘standard’); no density fitting (exact 4-center integrals) in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: single-point charges evaluated on the user-supplied aniline geometry (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/aniline/aniline.xyz); geometry was not re-optimized at DFT.
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model requested).
Charge & multiplicity: charge 0, multiplicity 1; cation (N-1) and anion (N+1) states automatically run as doublets (multiplicity 2).
Software/backend: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand “fukui”) calling PySCF for DFT SCF with B3LYP/def2-TZVP.
Convergence & caveats: SCF cycles for all three charge states converged; integrity.status = ok, integrity.trustworthy = true. Warnings reported by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.
Live log path: the calculation streamed to a .out file in the working directory while running (path provided during execution).

• Most electrophilic site (largest f⁺): atom 3 (C, index 2) with f⁺ = 2.2975867.
• Most nucleophilic site (largest f⁻): atom 1 (N, index 0) with f⁻ = 0.20452359.
